overall_assessment.fls_score_context: Your 401.3 FLS score represents solid intermediate-level performance, with excellent knot technique but room for improvement in precision and speed.
overall_assessment.session_headline: Strong knot sequence execution with proper hand switching - focus next on tightening suture placement accuracy and overall efficiency.

rubric_insights.observation: 210-second completion time is above the target range, with suture placement phase taking 70 seconds (33% of total time)
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Approximately 40-50 points lost due to extended completion time

rubric_insights.observation: 3.7mm total deviation from target marks (1.5mm + 2.2mm) indicates good but not precise placement
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: 3.7 penalty points directly subtracted from final score

rubric_insights.observation: Excellent knot sequence with proper surgeon's knot, correct hand switching, and secure final knot
rubric_insights.impact_on_score: Full points earned - no penalties for knot quality

technique_coaching.observation: In frames 3-4, needle approach to drain appears controlled but could be more decisive - some hesitation visible during positioning
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Hesitation during needle placement increases time and can lead to less precise targeting of anatomical landmarks
technique_coaching.correction: Commit to your needle entry angle earlier - once you've identified the target, drive through in one smooth motion rather than repositioning
technique_coaching.drill: Practice 20 needle passes through marked targets on Penrose drain, focusing on single decisive entry without repositioning

technique_coaching.observation: Frame 5 shows some unnecessary instrument repositioning during second pass setup
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Extra movements accumulate time and can disturb tissue positioning
technique_coaching.correction: Plan your second pass trajectory while completing the first pass - minimize instrument repositioning between passes
technique_coaching.drill: Practice dual-pass suture placement with focus on maintaining instrument position between passes - 10 repetitions

technique_coaching.observation: Good camera positioning maintained throughout knot tying sequence in frames 6-10
technique_coaching.why_it_matters: Consistent visualization prevents errors and maintains efficiency during complex maneuvers
technique_coaching.correction: Continue this excellent visualization technique - no changes needed

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Textbook surgeon's knot formation in frames 6-7 with proper double throw technique
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Surgeon's knots provide superior initial security and are essential for reliable tissue approximation
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your knot technique is excellent - this is advanced-level skill that many trainees struggle with

strengths_to_reinforce.observation: Perfect hand switching sequence maintained throughout all three throws in frames 8-10
strengths_to_reinforce.why_it_matters: Proper hand switching prevents knot slippage and ensures square knot formation
strengths_to_reinforce.encouragement: Your hand switching is automatic and correct - this demonstrates good muscle memory development

practice_plan.session_focus: Improve suture placement precision while maintaining excellent knot technique
practice_plan.warmup_drill: 5 minutes of needle loading and basic passes through unmarked Penrose drain to establish rhythm

primary_drill.name: Precision Placement Challenge
primary_drill.description: Mark two points 12mm apart on Penrose drain. Practice complete suture placement aiming for <1mm deviation from each mark. Focus on decisive needle entry without repositioning. Time each attempt.
primary_drill.success_criteria: Achieve <2mm total deviation on 10 out of 15 attempts, with completion time under 180 seconds

practice_plan.cooldown: Review your most accurate attempt - what felt different about your needle approach and targeting?
practice_plan.estimated_sessions_to_improvement: 2-3 focused sessions should bring placement deviation under 2mm consistently

progress_context.vs_baseline: Significant improvement from early 28.0 FLS score - knot technique has become consistently excellent
progress_context.vs_peak: Below your peak 600.0 FLS performances, but those had questionable confidence scores - this represents more reliable skill level
progress_context.milestone_note: Target sub-180s completion with <2mm total deviation to break into 450+ FLS range

coaching_limitations: Needle grasp point and exact entry angles not fully visible from this camera angle - recommendations based on visible hesitation patterns and movement efficiency
